components.name: Button
components.description: Triggers actions, submits forms, or navigates. The most fundamental interactive element in the system.
components.usage: Use buttons to initiate actions. Choose the variant that reflects the importance of the action.

props.name: label
props.label: Label
props.description: Button text

props.name: icon
props.label: Icon
props.description: Icon only, or paired with label on the left or right, not both sides

doList: Use default for primary actions, one per view
Use outline or ghost for secondary actions
Use destructive for irreversible actions like delete
dontList: Don't use more than one primary button per section
Don't use link variant for navigation within forms

components.name: Badge
components.description: Compact label for status, count, or category. Used inline alongside other content.
components.usage: Badges communicate status or categorization at a glance. Keep labels short.

props.name: label
props.label: Label
props.description: Badge text

doList: Keep text under 3 words
Use secondary for neutral/informational states
Use destructive sparingly for errors or warnings
dontList: Don't use as a button replacement
Don't stack more than 3 badges in a row

components.name: Table
components.description: Structured data display with sortable columns, optional footer, and title. Use for records that benefit from columnar scanning.
components.usage: Use tables to present structured data in rows and columns. Add a title above the table when context isn't obvious from surrounding copy.
doList: Label every column with a clear header
Right-align numeric columns so values line up for easy scanning
Use a footer row for totals or aggregate values
Place the table title above the table, not below it
dontList: Don't use a table for non-tabular data, use a list or card instead
Don't omit column headers unless the context makes each column self-evident

components.name: Data Table
components.description: Full-featured interactive table powered by TanStack Table v8. Supports sorting, pagination, global or column search, row selection, and column visibility, all themed via AG tokens.
components.usage: Use DataTable when your data needs sorting, filtering, or pagination. Define typed column defs with createColumnHelper, pass columns + data as props, and opt into features with boolean flags.

props.name: searchable
props.label: Searchable
props.description: Show a search input (global filter). Pass a column accessorKey string to filter a single column.

props.name: pagination
props.label: Pagination
props.description: Show pagination controls below the table

props.name: selectable
props.label: Selectable
props.description: Prepend a checkbox column for row selection

props.name: columnVisibility
props.label: Column Visibility
props.description: Show a column toggle dropdown in the toolbar

doList: Use createColumnHelper<YourType>() for full TypeScript inference on accessors and cell renderers
Define column defs outside the component (or with useMemo) to avoid re-creating the table instance on every render
Use the cell renderer to slot in Badges, Avatars, or action buttons, DataTable owns the layout, you own the content
Pass selectable + onSelectionChange together to act on the selected rows
dontList: Don't import from @tanstack/react-table directly for column defs, use the createColumnHelper re-export from @/components/ui/data-table so types stay consistent
Don't skip the title prop on DataTable, it provides an accessible label for screen readers via a visually hidden caption
Don't define columns inline in JSX without useMemo, it causes the table to recreate on every render

components.name: Card
components.description: Grouped container for related content. Cards establish visual hierarchy and organize information into scannable units.
components.usage: Use cards to group related content. Avoid nesting cards within cards.

props.name: layout
props.label: Layout
props.description: Stack content vertically or place media beside content.

props.name: imagePosition
props.label: Image
props.description: Optional image placement for the selected layout.

doList: Use consistent padding and spacing within cards
Group closely related items in the same card
dontList: Don't nest cards inside cards
Don't use cards as purely decorative containers

components.name: Input
components.description: Single-line text field for user data entry. Pairs with Label and supports disabled, readonly, and error states.
components.usage: Always pair inputs with a visible label. Use placeholder as a hint, not a label replacement.

props.name: placeholder
props.label: Placeholder
props.description: Leave blank to use the type default

props.name: helperText
props.label: Helper text
props.defaultValue: We'll never share your email with anyone else.

doList: Always pair with a visible label
Use placeholder text to show format (e.g. 'name@example.com')
Add helper text below the field for format hints or privacy notes
dontList: Don't use placeholder as the only label
Don't remove the label for 'space', use a visually hidden label instead

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Switches between related content sections without page navigation.
components.usage: Use tabs to organize content into parallel sections. Keep tab labels short and scannable.
doList: Use 2–5 tabs, more becomes hard to scan
Keep labels to 1–2 words
dontList: Don't use tabs for multi-step flows, use a stepper instead

components.name: Select
components.description: Dropdown for choosing one value from a list. Use when options exceed 5 items.
components.usage: Use Select when you have more than 5 options. For fewer choices, consider radio buttons.

components.name: Skeleton
components.description: Placeholder loading state that previews content shape while data loads.
components.usage: Use skeleton loaders for content that loads asynchronously. Match the shape of the content it replaces.

components.name: Area Chart
components.description: Interactive stacked area chart with a time-range filter. Shows trends across two data series with a tooltip and legend.
components.usage: Use area charts to visualize cumulative or stacked trends over time. The time-range selector lets users focus on 7-day, 30-day, or 90-day windows.

props.name: dateRange
props.label: Date range
props.defaultValue: Showing total visitors for the last 3 months

doList: Use for time-series data with two or more comparable series
Pair with a legend so each series is clearly labeled
Use the chart-* color tokens so charts respond to theme changes
dontList: Don't use stacked area when series overlap makes individual values hard to read, use a line chart instead
Don't skip axis labels, always make the time unit clear

components.name: Bar Chart
components.description: Simple bar chart displaying a single data series. Best for comparing discrete categories or time periods at a glance.
components.usage: Use bar charts when comparing a single metric across categories or time. Keep to six or fewer bars for clarity.
doList: Use chart-* color tokens so the bars adapt to theme changes
Label the axis so the unit of measurement is always clear
dontList: Don't use a bar chart for continuous data, use a line chart instead
Don't show more than ~12 bars without adding a scroll or filter

components.name: Bar Chart Stacked
components.description: Stacked bar chart comparing two data series with a legend. Ideal for showing part-to-whole relationships across time.
components.usage: Use when you need to show both the total and the breakdown of each segment. Pair with a legend so both series are clearly labeled.
doList: Always show a legend when using two or more series
Use chart-1 / chart-2 tokens to keep colors consistent across charts
dontList: Don't stack more than 3 series, the chart becomes unreadable
Don't use stacked bars when comparing individual series values is the primary goal, use a grouped bar instead

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Binary selection control. Available as a simple checkbox, with description text, or inside a bordered card row.
components.usage: Use checkboxes for multi-select options or toggling a single setting on/off. Use the card variant for settings panels.

props.name: variant
props.label: Variant
props.description: Visual style of the checkbox control

props.name: description
props.label: Description
props.defaultValue: You agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

doList: Always pair with a visible label to the right of the checkbox
Use for multi-select scenarios where multiple items can be chosen
Use the card variant for settings toggles where extra context is needed
dontList: Don't use a checkbox for mutually exclusive options, use a radio group
Don't use a checkbox as a primary call to action

components.name: Alert
components.description: Contextual message for informational, success, warning, or error states. Supports an optional dismiss control.
components.usage: Use alerts to surface important messages inline. Choose the variant that matches the message severity.

props.name: description
props.label: Description
props.defaultValue: You can add components to your app using the CLI.

doList: Match variant to severity: info for neutral, warning for caution, destructive for errors
Keep the message concise, one sentence title, one or two sentence description
Use AlertDismiss for dismissible alerts and remove the alert from the DOM on dismiss
dontList: Don't use alerts for success confirmations after actions, use a toast instead
Don't stack multiple alerts, consolidate into one or use a list
Don't hide critical errors behind dismiss without another way to recover the message

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Navigation control for moving through paged content. Includes previous/next arrows and numbered page buttons.
components.usage: Use pagination when content is split across pages. Show prev/next plus nearby page numbers. Disable prev on first page and next on last.
doList: Disable the Prev button on page 1 and Next on the last page
Highlight the current page clearly so users know where they are
Use ellipsis for large page counts rather than showing all numbers
dontList: Don't use pagination for small data sets, show all results instead
Don't hide pagination below the fold, place it at the bottom of the list or table

components.name: Button Group
components.description: A set of related buttons joined into a single connected control. Ideal for toggleable or segmented options.
components.usage: Use button groups for tightly related actions or view-switching (e.g. List / Grid). Avoid grouping more than 5 buttons.
doList: Use for 2–5 closely related options
Visually distinguish the active/selected button
Keep button labels short, 1–2 words each
dontList: Don't use more than 5 buttons in a group, use a Select instead
Don't mix icon-only and label buttons in the same group

components.name: Radio Group
components.description: Single-select control for mutually exclusive options. Shows all choices simultaneously for easy comparison.
components.usage: Use radio groups when users must choose exactly one option from a short list (2–6 items). For longer lists, use a Select.
doList: Use when there are 2–6 mutually exclusive options
Always pre-select the most common or safest option
Stack options vertically for easy scanning
dontList: Don't use for more than 6 options, use a Select instead
Don't use radio buttons for multi-select, use checkboxes

components.name: Sidebar
components.description: Composable, themeable sidebar with icon-collapse, Sheet mobile drawer, and SidebarGroup / SidebarMenu primitives matching shadcn.
components.usage: Wrap your layout in SidebarProvider. Compose Sidebar with SidebarHeader, SidebarContent (SidebarGroup + SidebarMenu), SidebarFooter, and SidebarRail. Wrap page content in SidebarInset.

props.name: showLogo
props.label: Logo
props.description: Show the brand logo and app name in the sidebar header.

props.name: showSearch
props.label: Search bar
props.description: Show the search input in the sidebar header.

doList: Wrap your root layout in SidebarProvider and place main content in SidebarInset
Use SidebarGroup + SidebarGroupLabel + SidebarMenu + SidebarMenuButton for navigation
Add SidebarRail as the last child of Sidebar for the collapse toggle rail
Place SidebarTrigger in the mobile header; use tooltip on menu buttons for collapsed labels
dontList: Don't use more than 3 nesting levels, flatten the structure instead
Don't put destructive actions (like 'Delete') in the nav, use a toolbar or context menu
Don't hardcode widths, use the --sidebar-width CSS variable so themes can override it

components.name: Donut Chart
components.description: Donut pie chart with five segments and a centred total-visitors label. Uses chart-* color tokens so it adapts to any active theme.
components.usage: Use donut charts to show part-to-whole relationships when you want to highlight a single summary value in the centre. Keep to 3–6 segments for readability.
doList: Limit segments to 3–6, more slices become difficult to distinguish
Use chart-* color tokens so segments adapt automatically when the theme changes
Add a centred label to surface the most important aggregate value
dontList: Don't use a pie or donut chart for comparing precise values, use a bar chart instead
Don't label individual slices with both a legend and inline text, pick one

components.name: Line Chart
components.description: Smooth line chart with dot markers for two series. Best for showing trends and changes over time.
components.usage: Use line charts to visualize how metrics change over a continuous range, such as daily signups or monthly revenue.
doList: Use dot markers to make individual data points easy to tap or hover
Use a natural curve (type="natural") for smoother visual flow
dontList: Don't use a line chart for unordered categories, use a bar chart instead
Don't plot too many series on one chart without a legend

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Collapsible panels for showing and hiding content sections. Smooth height animation on open/close.
components.usage: Use accordions to progressively disclose content and reduce visual clutter. Prefer single mode for FAQs.

props.name: multiple
props.label: Multiple
props.description: Allow multiple panels to be open simultaneously

doList: Use single mode for FAQs so only one answer is visible at a time
Keep trigger labels short and scannable
dontList: Don't hide critical information in accordions, use cards instead
Don't nest accordions inside accordions

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Circular user profile image with an initials fallback when the image is unavailable.
components.usage: Use avatars to represent users in comments, lists, and navigation. Always provide an alt text or initials fallback.
doList: Always provide a fallback with 1–2 initials
Use consistent sizing within a single list or row
dontList: Don't use avatars without an accessible label or alt text
Don't stack more than 5 overlapping avatars without a count badge

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.description: Accessible navigation trail showing the current page location within a hierarchy.
components.usage: Use breadcrumbs on pages deeper than 2 levels. The last item is always the current page and not a link.
doList: Keep each label short, 1 to 3 words
Always make the last item non-clickable to indicate the current page
dontList: Don't show breadcrumbs on top-level pages
Don't truncate mid-path, truncate from the beginning if space is limited

components.name: Carousel
components.description: Horizontally sliding content container with prev/next navigation and optional auto-play.
components.usage: Use carousels for a series of related visual items like testimonials or feature highlights. Keep to 3–5 slides.

props.name: loop
props.label: Loop
props.description: Wrap from last slide back to first

props.name: autoPlay
props.label: Auto Play
props.description: Automatically advance every 3 seconds

doList: Show navigation arrows so users can control the carousel
Keep slide count to 3–5 for best usability
dontList: Don't autoplay carousels that contain important information
Don't use carousels for primary navigation

components.name: Stepper
components.description: Horizontal step progress indicator for multi-step flows. Completed steps show a checkmark.
components.usage: Use steppers for linear multi-step processes like onboarding or checkout. Keep steps to 3–5.

props.name: currentStep
props.label: Current Step
props.description: Progress state — not started, in progress, or fully completed.

doList: Label each step clearly with 1–2 words
Always show the user's current position and completed steps
dontList: Don't use more than 5 steps, break long flows into stages instead
Don't use a stepper for non-linear flows, use tabs instead

components.name: Calendar
components.description: Full month calendar grid with date selection. Supports single date and range selection modes.
components.usage: Use the calendar component for date pickers and scheduling interfaces. Prefer the popover pattern for inline forms.

props.name: mode
props.label: Mode
props.description: Single date or date range selection

doList: Pre-select today's date when a current date is a sensible default
Highlight the selected range clearly in range mode
dontList: Don't use a full calendar for simple date-of-birth entry, use text inputs instead
Don't allow selection of disabled or unavailable dates without visual feedback

components.name: Command
components.description: Filterable command palette with grouped results. Use for search, navigation, and quick actions.
components.usage: Use command palettes for power-user search and navigation. Trigger with ⌘K or a dedicated search button.
doList: Group items into logical sections with clear headings
Show an empty state when no results match the query
dontList: Don't show more than 10 items per group without pagination
Don't use a command palette as the only search mechanism

components.name: Dialog
components.description: Modal dialog with overlay, scale animation, and accessible focus management via Base UI.
components.usage: Use dialogs for focused tasks that require user input before continuing. Keep content concise.
doList: Always provide a close button (X) and a Cancel action
Use for destructive confirmations and focused form flows
dontList: Don't stack dialogs, close the current one before opening another
Don't put scrolling content inside dialogs, use a drawer instead

components.name: Drawer
components.description: Slide-in panel from the right, left, or bottom edge. Built on Base UI Dialog for accessibility.
components.usage: Use drawers for secondary panels, detail views, and forms that need more vertical space than a dialog.

props.name: side
props.label: Side
props.description: Which edge the drawer slides in from

doList: Use right-side drawers for detail panels and forms
Use bottom drawers on mobile for sheet-style interactions
dontList: Don't use a drawer when a simple tooltip or popover would suffice
Don't open a drawer from within a dialog

components.name: Menu Bar
components.description: Horizontal menu bar with keyboard-navigable menus. Ideal for application-style top toolbars.
components.usage: Use a menu bar in desktop-style app shells where users need access to grouped commands across File, Edit, View, etc.

props.name: menuCount
props.label: Menu items
props.description: Number of top-level menus shown in the bar.

doList: Group related commands under clear top-level menu names
Show keyboard shortcuts next to menu items using MenuBarShortcut
dontList: Don't use a menu bar for top-level site navigation, use a top nav instead
Don't nest more than 2 levels of submenus

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Sticky top navigation bar with brand, breadcrumb trail, and right-aligned action area.
components.usage: Use the top nav as the global header in application shells. Pair with a side nav for deep hierarchies.

props.name: showLogo
props.label: Logo
props.description: Show the brand logo and name in the top nav.

props.name: showSearch
props.label: Search bar
props.description: Show the search input in the top nav actions area.

props.name: showBreadcrumbs
props.label: Breadcrumbs
props.description: Show the breadcrumb trail in the top nav.

props.name: breadcrumbLevels
props.label: Breadcrumb levels
props.description: Show a single current page or a three-level trail.

props.name: breadcrumbTitle
props.label: Breadcrumb title
props.description: Current page label when using one breadcrumb level.

props.name: breadcrumbTitle1
props.label: Level 1 title
props.description: First breadcrumb label in a three-level trail.

props.name: breadcrumbTitle2
props.label: Level 2 title
props.description: Middle breadcrumb label in a three-level trail.

props.name: breadcrumbTitle3
props.label: Level 3 title
props.description: Current page label in a three-level trail.

doList: Keep the brand area minimal, logo or name only
Use the breadcrumb to orient users within the app hierarchy
dontList: Don't add more than 3 action buttons to the right slot
Don't use the top nav for marketing pages, use a dedicated site header

components.name: Resizable
components.description: Drag-to-resize panel layout with a visual handle divider. Supports horizontal and vertical splits.
components.usage: Use resizable panels for IDE-style layouts or side-by-side comparison views where users need to adjust proportions.

props.name: direction
props.label: Direction
props.description: Split panels side-by-side or stacked vertically.

doList: Set a minSize on each panel to prevent them from collapsing completely
Show a visible grip icon on the handle so the interaction is discoverable
dontList: Don't use resizable panels on mobile, use stacked layouts instead
Don't nest resizable groups more than 2 levels deep
